subject: Computer Networking
title: NTP: Keeping Clocks in Sync
description: A quick, practical guide to NTP: Keeping Clocks in Sync.

hook.text: Your phone, laptop, and server all show the exact same time. They aren't guessing — they're using NTP.

buildup.text: NTP — Network Time Protocol — synchronizes clocks across the internet to within a few milliseconds.

discovery.text: It works by exchanging timestamps with reference servers. Math accounts for network delay in each direction.

twist.text: Accurate time is critical for security. TLS certificates, log analysis, and two-factor auth all depend on it.

climax.text: Stratum 0 clocks use atomic oscillators or GPS. NTP cascades that accuracy down through layers of servers.

punchline.text: NTP keeps the digital world on the same clock.

quiz.question: Why is accurate time critical for network security?
options: Certificates, logs, and authentication depend on synchronized clocks
Faster clocks encrypt data more quickly
